response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of broad full-stack experience with strong alignment to the primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, and React are all present, and his current role at re:cinq explicitly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks, which is a direct match for the AI agent development requirement. His TDD-heavy background, testing advocacy, and ETH Zürich CS degree further strengthen the case. The main gaps are the absence of Nest.js (the core backend framework), no explicit mention of Kotlin or Python (both listed as required proficiencies), and no Vitest/Jest/Playwright experience called out directly — though his track record across many languages and his own test framework authorship suggest these are learnable gaps rather than disqualifying ones.
strengths: Directly relevant AI agent and MCP framework experience in current role at re:cinq, matching the job's emerging AI development requirement
Deep TypeScript/Node.js/React full-stack background with cloud (AWS CDK), Docker, and Kubernetes exposure across multiple senior roles
Strong testing culture — TDD practitioner, created multiple testing libraries, ran testing workshops; fits a team that lists Vitest as a primary tool
concerns: No mention of Nest.js anywhere on the resume, which is the listed primary backend framework for this role
Kotlin and Python are absent from stated skills despite both being listed as required proficiencies in the job description
No explicit reference to Vitest, Jest, or Playwright; testing expertise is demonstrable but tied to D-language tooling rather than the JS/TS ecosystem
